title: carlina libanotica subsp. microcephala
intro: Carlina libanotica subsp. microcephala is a subspecies of the Carlina libanotica plant, commonly known as the Syrian scabious, which belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in the Mediterranean region, due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Carlina libanotica subsp. microcephala include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Carlina libanotica subsp. microcephala can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
